question_text: A student measures a marble and finds it has a mass of 8.5 g and a volume of 5 cm³. Calculate the density of the marble.
explanation: Use the formula density = mass ÷ volume. Density = 8.5 g ÷ 5 cm³ = 1.7 g/cm³. This is a straightforward one-step calculation using the density formula.
